behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at the model-output-to-index-schema handoff, not a single pure function. LLM-DEP/AI-DEP applies because correctness depends on a real embedding model output shape; a stubbed vector can hide provider/model dimension drift. BIDIRECTIONAL-REJECTION-GATE: reject System (L-1) because behavior text says "embedding vector produced" and correctness depends on AI model output content, not only transport/HTTP. Reject Workflow (L+1) because behavior text covers one model step (embedding) and no multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real embedding model for fixed queries against the configured production-like model id, then assert returned vector length equals the live index dimension read from the vector DB schema endpoint/config. Fail on any mismatch.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior is about module handoff (search service + vector repo + score mapping), not just one local helper. REAL-INFRA: no required OS/network/subprocess boundary if using an in-process/local vector fixture. BIDIRECTIONAL-REJECTION-GATE: reject Unit (L-1) because behavior text says "similarity search returns results," which requires wiring components, not isolated function-only logic. Reject System (L+1) because behavior text only requires ordering by cosine score and does not require real HTTP/subprocess/OS effects.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a local/in-process vector store fixture with known vectors and expected cosine scores, run the search module end-to-end in process, and assert returned scores are monotonically non-increasing and IDs match expected order.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness directly depends on real LLM judgment of "nuanced intent." A deterministic mock would always pass/fail trivially and cannot validate steering/semantic reordering quality. BIDIRECTIONAL-REJECTION-GATE: reject System (L-1) because behavior text explicitly targets "LLM re-ranking" quality, not just infrastructure around the call. Reject Workflow (L+1) because this behavior isolates one agentic decision point (single re-ranker step), not multiple agentic stages chained.
behaviors.test_description: Use a curated set of nuanced queries with ambiguous candidate results, run raw similarity ranking then real LLM re-ranker, and assert at least one pairwise inversion occurs in predefined cases where contextual intent should override pure vector similarity.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: AI-DEP: deciding "no close matches" vs "low-relevance noise" depends on embedding-space semantics from a real model; stubs can hide real false-positive retrieval behavior. BIDIRECTIONAL-REJECTION-GATE: reject System (L-1) because behavior text is about relevance quality ("low-relevance noise"), not merely network/process behavior. Reject Workflow (L+1) because this behavior can be validated at the retrieval decision boundary and does not require multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run real embeddings for out-of-domain/no-match queries against a realistic corpus, apply production similarity thresholding, and assert API returns empty results (or explicit no-results response) with no below-threshold items leaked.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA + MULTI-STEP: this is full-pipeline SLO across sequential agentic steps (embedding call + vector search + LLM re-rank) plus service overhead. Any mocked stage can mask true median latency distribution. BIDIRECTIONAL-REJECTION-GATE: reject Agentic (L-1) because behavior text says "end-to-end latency ... to returning re-ranked results," which requires the multi-step pipeline, not a single model call. L+1 not applicable because Workflow is the highest defined level.
behaviors.test_description: Execute production-like load tests through the real REST endpoint with real embedding and LLM providers and real vector DB, collect end-to-end latencies for representative median-traffic queries, and assert p50 < 2.0s over a stable sample window.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Workflow is required because the assertion is explicitly on full sequential pipeline latency, not a single stage.
